mnemonics: HAPPY CONVEX: Hyperplanes Always Partition Properly, Yielding Convex Optimal Nifty EXtremes
keyPoints: A set is convex if the line segment between any two points in the set lies entirely within the set.
A function is convex if the line segment between any two points on its graph lies on or above the graph.
A separating hyperplane exists between two disjoint convex sets, providing a linear inequality that divides them.
commonMistakes: Assuming all functions with a single minimum are convex (e.g., f(x)=x^4 is convex, but f(x)=x^3 is not).
Confusing a supporting hyperplane (touching the set's boundary) with a strictly separating hyperplane (a gap exists).
Assuming that if a hyperplane can be drawn between two sets, they must both be convex (non-convex sets can also sometimes be separated).
